steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "When Fiji joined Interpol—the International Criminal Police ______ in 1971, the Fiji National Police..." The phrase "the International Criminal Police Organization" is a parenthetical definition of "Interpol," opened with an em dash (—) before "the International." Rule: a parenthetical opened with an em dash must be closed with a matching em dash.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Organization—: "...Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization— in 1971..." Closes the em-dash pair correctly; the parenthetical is properly enclosed. ✓
  • Choice B — Organization:: "...Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization: in 1971..." A colon after "Organization" does not close the opening em dash — the pair is broken, and a colon introducing "in 1971" makes no sense. ✗
  • Choice C — Organization: "...Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization in 1971..." No punctuation to close the em-dash pair; "in 1971" appears to modify "Organization" rather than the main clause. ✗
  • Choice D — Organization,: "...Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization, in 1971..." A comma does not close an em dash; the paired structure is broken. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
